commit.message:
Disable kitty keyboard `REPORT_EVENT_TYPES` flag (#264)

The kitty keyboard enhancement flag `REPORT_EVENT_TYPES` causes
terminals to emit separate press/release/repeat events. This removes
that flag so only key press events are received.

### Changes

- **`PushKeyboardEnhancementFlags`**: removed `REPORT_EVENT_TYPES` from
the flags bitmask
- **Event loop**: simplified key handler — dropped the `match key.kind`
dispatch since release events no longer arrive
- **`on_keyrelease`**: removed the method (was only used to toggle mouse
capture on Alt release in Simple mode)
- **Stale comment**: removed reference to `on_keyrelease` in the Alt
press handler

```rust
// Before
crossterm::event::PushKeyboardEnhancementFlags(
    KeyboardEnhancementFlags::DISAMBIGUATE_ESCAPE_CODES
        | KeyboardEnhancementFlags::REPORT_EVENT_TYPES   // <-- removed
        | KeyboardEnhancementFlags::REPORT_ALTERNATE_KEYS
        | KeyboardEnhancementFlags::REPORT_ALL_KEYS_AS_ESCAPE_CODES
)
```

commit.message:
fix: update bench report parser for new multi-method bench_navigator output (#95)

* Initial plan

* fix: update generate_bench_report.py for new bench_navigator output format

The bench_navigator.cc output was restructured from a single-line-per-fixture
ray-only format to a multi-method per-fixture block format. Update the parser
and renderer in generate_bench_report.py to match:

- Section header: '=== Fixture Navigation Benchmark Results ===' (was 'Ray')
- Count line: 'Rays: N  |  Inside points: N  |  Safety points: N' (was 'Rays per fixture: N')
- Per-fixture: multi-line blocks with 6 method rows each
- Aggregate: per-method rows with fixed-width column layout

The renderer now shows a per-method aggregate table and a per-fixture table
with DistanceToIn/Out timing and total mismatches across all methods.
Add _parse_timing_value / _format_timing helpers to reduce duplication.

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: restructure per-fixture table with per-method columns and compact cells

Each of the 6 navigation methods (DTI/DTO(p,v), Exit normals, Inside(p),
DTI(p), DTO(p), SN(p)) now gets its own column in the per-fixture table.
Cell format: "native → imported (ratio) [✅]" or "[N ⚠️]" for mismatches.
Exit normals (no timing) shows only the mismatch indicator.

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add legend paragraph before per-fixture results table

Explains cell format, mismatch indicators, Exit normals caveat,
column abbreviations, and expected-failure marker.

Co-authored-by: wdconinc <4656391+wdconinc@users.noreply.github.com>

- Emit stderr warning on malformed aggregate rows instead of silent pass
- Correct Exit normals legend (timing is absent because normals are computed
  as part of DistanceToOut, not because of missing rays)
- Render missing method data as '---' instead of defaulting to [✅]

commit.message:
Implement IMPALA ResNet with CoordConv and multi-head attention (#180)

* Add IMPALA ResNet with CoordConv and spatial attention pooling

New visual encoder architecture for full-screen RGB observation:

Architecture:
- ResBlock: Pre-activation residual block (ReLU→Conv→ReLU→Conv + skip)
- ImpalaResNet: 3 residual stacks (5→16→32→32) with CoordConv buffers
  and spatial self-attention pooling, producing a 256-dim feature vector
- SpatialAttentionPool: 1×1 conv Q/K/V projections, scaled dot-product
  attention over all spatial positions, weighted-sum pooling
- ImpalaCombinedExtractor: ImpalaResNet + entity attention + info = 335-dim
- ImpalaSharedAgent / ImpalaMultiHeadAgent: New Network subclasses with
  forward_with_attention() for visualization

All spatial dimensions are dynamic (works for both 168×240 cropped and
176×256 full overscan modes). CoordConv channels registered as buffers.

Observation:
- New obs_kind='full-rgb': HUD trimmed, RGB (no grayscale), no viewport
  crop, frame_stack=1. Output shape: (3, H, W) normalized [0,1].

Config:
- New model-kinds in triforce.yaml: impala-shared, impala-multihead

Debugger:
- ATTENTION overlay flag in OverlayFlags enum
- Attention heatmap checkbox in View menu
- JET colormap heatmap overlay on gameplay area
- Attention weights fetched from model via forward_with_attention()

Tests:
- 28 new tests covering ResBlock, SpatialAttentionPool, ImpalaResNet,
  ImpalaSharedAgent, ImpalaMultiHeadAgent, registration, save/load,
  both screen sizes, CoordConv buffer properties

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Auto-detect obs_kind from saved models in evaluate/diagnose/debug

Add infer_obs_kind() to observation_wrapper.py that determines the
observation kind (viewport/full-rgb/gameplay) and frame_stack from
the saved observation space shape. This removes the need for explicit
--obs-kind parameters when loading existing models.

- evaluate.py: uses infer_obs_kind(network.observation_space)
- diagnose.py: uses infer_obs_kind(metadata['obs_space']) at all 3 sites
- environment_bridge.py: infers from model metadata, returns obs_space
- train.py: infers from --load model when available, auto-detects from
  model_kind name for new training (impala-* defaults to full-rgb)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix IMPALA OOM: auto-increase minibatches for spatial attention

The 630-token spatial attention matrix (630×630 × batch) causes OOM
with the default 4 minibatches (minibatch_size=1024). Add
recommended_minibatches=16 to IMPALA agents, which PPO respects
unless explicitly overridden. This reduces minibatch size to 256,
cutting attention memory from ~1.9GB to ~0.5GB per minibatch.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add attention dropout to prevent attention collapse

SpatialAttentionPool now applies dropout (p=0.1) to the attention
weights during training. This prevents the model from fixating on a
single spatial position (e.g., the CoordConv corner) and encourages
it to learn content-dependent attention patterns.

Note: this changes the model architecture, so existing checkpoints
are not compatible — training must restart from scratch.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Log attention entropy and top-1 weight to tensorboard

Adds 'attention/entropy' and 'attention/top1_weight' metrics for
IMPALA models. Entropy near ln(N) means uniform attention (healthy),
near 0 means collapsed to one position (bad). Top-1 weight directly
shows the fraction of attention on the most-attended position.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add tensorboard metrics docs and per-scenario log directories

- docs/tensorboard-metrics.md: Documents all RL metrics with healthy
  ranges and warning thresholds (value_loss, approx_kl, clipfrac,
  explained_variance, entropy, attention health, etc.)
- train.py: Each scenario in a training circuit now gets its own
  tensorboard log directory (logs/<scenario-name>/) instead of all
  scenarios writing to a single flat logs/ directory.
- ml_ppo.py: Add set_log_dir() to switch SummaryWriter mid-training.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add TrainingCallback protocol and Rich TUI for training

- Create TrainingCallback protocol in triforce/ml_ppo_callback.py
- Refactor PPO to use callback instead of tqdm/tensorboard
- Remove tensorboard and tqdm imports from triforce library
- Build Rich-based TUI display in train.py with:
  - Aligned progress bars per scenario using ▄ lower half block
  - All circuit scenarios listed upfront (✔ completed, active, pending)
  - Total progress bar across all scenarios
  - Live metrics table (game metrics + optimization stats)
- Move tensorboard logging to TrainingDisplay callback in train.py
- Export TrainingCallback from triforce package
- Add rich to requirements.txt
- Update test assertions for new callback API

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add ETA, rolling SPS, and reorganized metrics to training TUI

- ETA on total progress bar using rolling SPS over last 25k iterations
- Metrics reorganized into three groups:
  - Performance: progress/p75, rewards, success-rate, rewards-since-last
  - Entropy/Attention: attention/entropy, attention/top1_weight,
    entropy/action_type, entropy/direction
  - Losses: value_loss, policy_loss, entropy, approx_kl, clipfrac,
    explained_variance
- rewards-since-last computed as delta between consecutive rewards averages
- ETA hidden until 25k iteration warm-up period completes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove entity positions and obs image from debug panel

- Remove rel_x/rel_y from entity observations (ENTITY_FEATURES 9→7)
- Remove POSITION_SCALE constant from observation_wrapper
- Remove ObsImageWidget and SmallArrowWidget from observation_panel
- Remove obs_image from ObservationPanel (no longer needed with full-screen)
- Update EntityAttentionEncoder default continuous_features to 7
- Clean up tests and imports

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add pause/resume support to training TUI

- Press 'p' to toggle pause during training
- State machine: running → requesting → paused → running
  - Double-press cancels before pause takes effect
- Pauses at natural training boundaries (after optimize step)
- Status messages: 'Pausing after next training point...' / 'Paused (press p to resume)'
- ETA freezes while paused (shows last computed value)
- SPS tracking uses pause-adjusted time so pauses don't inflate ETA
- Background keyboard listener thread using raw terminal mode
- Terminal state restored on completion
- Add check_pause() to TrainingCallback protocol

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add graceful quit (q key) to training TUI

- Press q once: 'Press q again to end training early.'
- Press q again: stops at next sync point, saves model
- Any other key cancels quit confirmation
- check_pause() now returns bool (True=continue, False=stop)
- PPO training loops break on False return
- q during pause: resumes and stops immediately
- Stop message shown in red: 'Stopping after next training point...'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Vectorize attention heatmap rendering (~20x faster)

Replace per-pixel Python loop (53K iterations) with a precomputed
256-entry JET colormap LUT and vectorized NumPy indexing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix observation panel spacing: stack tightly, entities fill remaining space

- Reduce layout spacing from 4 to 2
- Set stretch=0 on title, circles, booleans, entity header
- Replace setFixedHeight(140) on entity scroll with setMinimumHeight(100)
  so entities expand to fill available space

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add multi-head spatial attention (4 heads)

- SpatialAttentionPool now splits Q/K/V across num_heads=4 independent
  heads, each computing its own attention pattern in parallel
- Attention weights shape: (batch, num_heads, H, W) instead of (batch, H, W)
- Per-head entropy and top-1 weight logged to tensorboard
- Debugger: [ and ] keys cycle through Combined/Head 1-4 display
- Head label shown on game view when attention overlay is active

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Merge tui branch and add per-head attention metrics to TUI

Merge origin/tui (Rich TUI, pause/resume, ETA, graceful quit).
Add per-head attention entropy and top-1 weight to TUI display
for all 4 attention heads.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
bench/tests: skip NIST CTC navigation while assessing slowdown and G4Exceptions (#93)

Commit 3f59ca6 (PR #87) made CompareFixtureRays/Inside/Safety call
BuildNativeSolidForRequest, which for G4OCCTSolid fixtures creates a
second G4OCCTSolid from the same STEP file instead of skipping.
bench_navigator therefore now runs 2048-ray navigation over all 11 NIST
CTC AP203 compound-solid fixtures.  These are large, complex assemblies
(nist-ctc-02: 24 k STEP lines; nist-ctc-07: 7.8 k lines) whose
BRepClass3d_SolidClassifier and IntCurvesFace_ShapeIntersector calls are
very slow and trigger G4Exceptions from degenerate or near-surface
topology, pushing the build-test-benchmark CI job from ~10 min to 5+
hours and causing test_geometry_validation/nist-ctc-02-v1 and
nist-ctc-07-v1 to hit the CTest timeout.

Note that test_geometry_validation.cc already has an
IsImportedSelfComparisonFixture guard that correctly skips ray and
safety navigation for G4OCCTSolid fixtures; bench_navigator.cc lacked
an equivalent guard.

Changes:
- bench_navigator.cc: add IsImportedSelfComparisonFixture helper and
  guard the CompareFixtureRays/Inside/Safety block with it.  Geometry
  import and volume coverage remain via test_geometry_validation
  (geometry checks) and test_nist_ctc_inside_volume.
- CMakeLists.txt: disable all 11 test_geometry_validation/nist-ctc-*
  sub-tests until per-fixture timeouts are in place.

Both changes are explicitly temporary; they will be re-enabled once the
OCCT navigation performance on AP203 compound solids is understood.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement tilde expansion (#260)

Tilde expansion was essentially unimplemented: `~<TAB>` fell through to
`FilenameExpansion` and tried to glob `~*` in the current directory (no
results), and the `TildeExpansion` completion path was hard-disabled
with `false &&`.

## Changes

- **`tab_completion_context.rs`**: Remove `false &&` guard — `~username`
(no `/`) now routes to `TildeExpansion` instead of `FilenameExpansion`

- **`tab_completion.rs`** — rewrite `tab_complete_tilde_expansion`:
  - `~<TAB>` → returns `~/`
- `~partial<TAB>` → lists `/home/` entries + checks `/root`, returns
`~username/` (previously returned raw `/home/username/` paths)

- **`mod.rs`**: Add tilde tooltip in `wordinfo_fn` — hovering over a
tilde word (e.g. `~/docs`) shows `~/docs=/home/user/docs`, consistent
with the existing `$VAR=value` env var tooltip

Note: `~/path<TAB>` (contains `/`) was already handled correctly via
`FilenameExpansion` → `PathPatternExpansion` →
`bash_funcs::expand_filename`.

commit.message:
Charts: Extract shared ChartLayout component for chart+legend layout (#47554)

* Charts: Add ChartLayout component for shared chart+legend layout

Introduces a ChartLayout component that encapsulates the shared
Stack + legend positioning pattern used by all chart components.
Includes comprehensive tests covering legend positioning, visibility
management, ref forwarding, and composition API slot rendering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Export ChartLayoutProps type from barrel

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Migrate LineChart to use ChartLayout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Migrate BarChart to use ChartLayout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Migrate PieChart to use ChartLayout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Migrate PieSemiCircleChart to use ChartLayout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Migrate LeaderboardChart to use ChartLayout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Restore responsive/loading classes in LeaderboardChart empty state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: PieChart uses ChartLayout render prop for measurement

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Add render prop and measurement support to ChartLayout

ChartLayout now accepts children as either ReactNode or a render prop
receiving ContentMeasurements (contentRef, contentWidth, contentHeight,
isMeasured). This allows charts to consolidate their useElementSize
calls into the shared layout component.

Adds waitForMeasurement prop that hides the layout until content is
measured. The legacy isWaitingForMeasurement prop is preserved as
deprecated for backward compatibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Fix PieChart SingleChartContext.Provider placement

Move SingleChartContext.Provider to wrap ChartLayout (outside) so
composition API legends in legend slots can access the chart context.
Keep inner provider inside the render prop for measured dimensions.

Remove waitForMeasurement since PieChart uses fallback dimensions
(300x300) when unmeasured and doesn't need to hide.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Charts: Migrate LineChart, BarChart, PieSemiCircleChart to ChartLayout render prop

Remove individual useElementSize calls from each chart and use
ChartLayout's render prop to receive content measurements instead.

Pattern: outer SingleChartContext.Provider with fallback dimensions
(for composition API legends in layout slots), inner provider inside
render prop with measured dimensions (for chart internals).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add changelog entries.

* test(charts): Simplify ChartLayout DOM order assertions

Replace the expectDomOrder helper with direct compareDocumentPosition
assertions. The helper required eslint-disable no-bitwise for the
masking check; inline expect(...).toBe(Node.DOCUMENT_POSITION_FOLLOWING)
is clearer and avoids the suppression.

Made-with: Cursor

* Charts: Extract shared CSS into ChartLayout, use Stack for pie centering

ChartLayout now wraps render prop children in a content div with shared
flex styles (flex: 1; min-height: 0; min-width: 0), removing duplicated
svg-wrapper rules from line-chart, bar-chart, pie-chart, and
pie-semi-circle-chart SCSS modules.

Charts no longer need to attach contentRef — ChartLayout handles
measurement internally via the content wrapper div.

Replace custom flex centering CSS in pie-chart and pie-semi-circle-chart
svg-wrappers with Stack components using align/justify props, per
project guidelines preferring layout primitives over ad-hoc flex rules.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(charts): Remove redundant flex and inline styles from pie charts

The pie-chart class had `display: flex; flex-direction: column;
align-items: center` which duplicated what the Stack component
already provides. The `align-items: center` also prevented the
chart-layout content area from stretching to full width.

Similarly, the centering Stack inside both pie charts had inline
`style={{ width: '100%', height: '100%' }}` that is unnecessary
now that ChartLayout's content wrapper handles flex sizing.

Also removes hardcoded `size` props from two pie chart stories
that were masking the responsive sizing behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore(charts): Remove superpowers plan from tracked files

Superpowers directories are now ignored via .git/info/exclude.
Removing the previously tracked plan file so it stays local.

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Move ChartLayout to charts/private

ChartLayout is internal infrastructure consumed only by chart
components, not a public API. Moving it alongside other internal
modules (chart-composition, single-chart-context, with-responsive)
makes its scope clear and avoids exposing it in the components
directory.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(charts): Pass measured chart height to SingleChartContext in LineChart

SingleChartContext.chartHeight was set to the full container height
(including legend space), but AnnotationsOverlay uses this value to
size its SVG overlay. This caused misaligned annotations when a legend
was present because the overlay was sized larger than the actual chart
drawing area.

Lift the measured content height from the ChartLayout render prop into
state so the context provider reflects the true drawable area.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(charts): Add full dimensions to pie chart centering Stack

The centering Stack inside the ChartLayout render prop had no explicit
dimensions, so justify="center" had no effect because the Stack
collapsed to content height. Adding width/height 100% makes the Stack
fill the measured content area, enabling proper vertical centering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(charts): Fix misleading isMeasured docstring in ChartLayout

The docstring claimed isMeasured is "always true when
waitForMeasurement is not set", but isMeasured derives from
contentHeight > 0, which is false on initial render before
ResizeObserver fires regardless of waitForMeasurement.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(charts): Add ChartLayout render-prop and waitForMeasurement tests

Cover the render-prop measurement behavior (function-as-children
receives contentWidth/contentHeight/isMeasured) and verify that
waitForMeasurement takes precedence over the legacy
isWaitingForMeasurement prop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(charts): Remove legacy isWaitingForMeasurement prop from ChartLayout

No callers pass isWaitingForMeasurement as a prop to ChartLayout —
line-chart and bar-chart only use it as a local variable name. Simplify
the visibility logic to only support waitForMeasurement and update
tests accordingly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(charts): Use useEffect callback instead of setState during render

Calling setMeasuredChartHeight inside ChartLayout's render prop
triggered a React warning: "Cannot update a component while rendering
a different component". Add an onContentHeightChange callback prop to
ChartLayout that fires via useEffect after measurement, avoiding the
cross-component state update during render.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(charts): Document waitForMeasurement render-prop requirement

waitForMeasurement only works with render-prop children because
contentRef is only attached in that code path. With plain ReactNode
children, isMeasured stays false and the layout remains permanently
hidden. Add JSDoc warning to prevent silent misuse.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(charts): Remove unused chartWidth/chartHeight from pie and leaderboard context

Pie, semi-circle, and leaderboard charts don't have consumers that
read chartWidth/chartHeight from SingleChartContext. Passing zeros was
misleading. Make the fields optional on ChartInstanceContextValue and
omit them from charts that don't provide meaningful values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(charts): Pass measured chart height to SingleChartContext in BarChart

Same pattern as LineChart: track the measured content height via
onContentHeightChange callback so SingleChartContext.chartHeight
reflects the actual drawable area, not the full container height.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(charts): Auto-hide ChartLayout during measurement for render-prop children

Replace the opt-in waitForMeasurement prop with automatic behavior:
when children is a render prop, ChartLayout applies visibility:hidden
until contentHeight > 0. This prevents layout shift consistently
across all charts without each chart needing to opt in.

Plain ReactNode children are unaffected since they don't depend on
measured dimensions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(charts): Simplify measurement gating, mock element size in tests

ChartLayout now auto-hides render-prop children until measured, so
individual charts no longer need their own isWaitingForMeasurement
logic. Simplify bar/line charts to gate on chartHeight > 0 directly
and remove the now-unused hasLegend variables.

Add a getBoundingClientRect + ResizeObserver mock to the test setup so
useElementSize returns non-zero dimensions in JSDOM. Without this, the
auto-hide visibility guard keeps charts permanently hidden in tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(charts): Remove redundant visx tooltip mock

The setup-visx-tooltip-mock.js mock overrode useTooltipInPortal to
provide non-zero containerBounds because JSDOM's getBoundingClientRect
returns zeros. Now that setup-element-size-mock.js globally mocks
getBoundingClientRect to return 800x400, the visx mock is redundant —
react-use-measure (used internally by useTooltipInPortal) gets non-zero
values directly from the source.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style(charts): Move pie chart centering styles to CSS modules

Inline styles bypass the CSS module system and make overrides harder.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(charts): Fix 6 test failures from element size mock

The setup-element-size-mock.js introduced in e242b7d had three flaws:

1. Accessed Element.prototype unconditionally, crashing the SSR test
   (jest-environment node) with "Element is not defined"

2. Returned hardcoded 800×400 for ALL zero-sized elements, overriding
   explicit dimensions (e.g. Sparkline at 100×40 got height 400)

3. The visx tooltip mock removed in 92201ce was NOT redundant —
   react-use-measure has a mounted.current guard that prevents state
   updates when ResizeObserver fires synchronously during the ref
   callback, leaving containerBounds at {0,0,...} and suppressing
   all tooltips

Fixes:
- Guard Element access with typeof check for SSR compatibility
- Walk up DOM tree for ancestor inline style dimensions instead of
  hardcoded fallback, so charts with explicit sizes measure correctly
- Restore visx tooltip mock with updated documentation explaining
  the react-use-measure timing issue
- Override getBoundingClientRect locally in chart-layout visibility
  test to simulate unmeasured state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(charts): Guard onContentHeightChange for render-prop mode

When children is a plain ReactNode, the content measurement ref is
never attached, so contentHeight stays 0. Firing
onContentHeightChange(0) in that case is misleading for callers who
wire up the callback without realizing it only produces meaningful
values in render-prop mode.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(charts): Wrap mock override in try/finally

Ensures the global getBoundingClientRect mock is restored even if
an assertion throws, preventing leak into subsequent tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Only call onContentHeightChange if measured

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(charts): Enhance early return condition in LineChartAnnotationsOverlay

Updated the early return condition to include checks for chartWidth and chartHeight, ensuring that the component only renders when all necessary dimensions are available. This prevents potential rendering issues when the chart is not properly sized.

* fix(charts): Pass args through in composition legend stories

Composition legend stories for bar, pie, donut, and semi-circle
were cherry-picking individual props instead of spreading args.
This meant Storybook controls like showLegend had no effect,
preventing dual legend rendering (showLegend + composition
<Chart.Legend>) that works in line chart and leaderboard stories.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: replace slide 6 single-line SVG with dual-curve lifecycle visual and add cited sources

The individual slide page for Slide 6 (Technology Lifecycle Positioning) was
using an outdated single-line SVG that didn't match the dual-curve visual used
in slide-render.tsx and presentation-visuals.tsx. This replaces it with the
correct dual-curve chart showing Innovation Potential (cyan dashed) vs Adoption
Risk (amber solid) with the Leading Edge → Mainstream target zone.

Also enriches the lifecycle stage definitions in the markdown content with
real citations from Rogers (Diffusion of Innovations), Moore (Crossing the
Chasm), Gartner Hype Cycle, NIST SP 800-160, Microsoft Lifecycle Policy,
and CISA KEV catalog.

* feat: expand slide 6 with dual-curve analysis and real-world lifecycle examples

Add three substantive sections to slide 6 (Technology Lifecycle Positioning):

1. "Reading the Chart" — explains the dual-curve relationship between
   innovation potential and adoption risk, citing Christensen (Innovator's
   Dilemma), Gartner failure rates at Innovation Trigger, NIST Cybersecurity
   Framework 2.0 on aging infrastructure risk, and Rogers' 68% adoption
   window for early/late majority.

2. "Real-World Examples" — a 12-row table mapping concrete technologies
   (WebTransport, PQC, Rust, Deno/Bun, Node.js, React, PostgreSQL, jQuery,
   AngularJS, Windows 10, CentOS 7, Python 2.7) to their lifecycle stage
   with evidence from Stack Overflow 2024, DB-Engines, Gartner, NIST,
   Microsoft, Red Hat, CISA KEV, and others.

3. Expanded sources list (18 references) and three new speaker notes
   connecting the examples to the lifecycle model.

* feat: add lifecycle timeline charts for hardware, software, and supply chain examples

Add three new optional deep-dive slides (27-29) with proportional
timeline bar charts showing real technologies progressing through each
lifecycle phase. Each chart includes sourced phase durations that
explain why real adoption curves are asymmetric (imperfect S-curves):

- Slide 27: Hardware (HDDs) — 77-year lifecycle, right-skewed curve
  with 30-year mainstream plateau and rapid SSD displacement
- Slide 28: Software (Adobe Flash) — 25-year lifecycle, left-skewed
  with compressed 1-year EOL cliff after Apple/HTML5 displacement
- Slide 29: Supply Chain (Barcodes) — 83-year lifecycle, extremely
  long 22-year bleeding edge due to infrastructure dependencies

Includes dark-theme presentation visuals, light-theme landing page

commit.message:
Fix: Smart mouse mode re-enables after explicit user disable (#257)

In Smart mode, any keypress unconditionally re-enabled mouse capture —
meaning if a user explicitly toggled mouse off (via Escape), it would
immediately re-enable on the next keypress. The 500ms periodic re-enable
timer had the same problem.

## Changes

**`src/mouse_state.rs`**
- Added `explicitly_disabled_by_user: bool` field to `MouseState`
- `toggle()` now sets/clears this flag — auto-disable (scroll, viewport)
does **not** affect it
- Added `is_explicitly_disabled_by_user()` getter
- `check_reenable_timer()` skips re-enable when flag is set (timer is
still consumed to prevent repeat firings)

**`src/app/mod.rs`**
- Smart mode `on_keypress` now gates the auto re-enable on
`!is_explicitly_disabled_by_user()`
- Escape now toggles mouse capture in Smart mode (previously Simple mode
only)

```rust
// Before: unconditional re-enable on every keypress
if self.settings.mouse_mode == MouseMode::Smart {
    self.mouse_state.enable("smart mode: keypress detected");
    self.mouse_state.cancel_reenable();
}

// After: respects explicit user disable
if self.settings.mouse_mode == MouseMode::Smart {
    if !self.mouse_state.is_explicitly_disabled_by_user() {
        self.mouse_state.enable("smart mode: keypress detected");
    }
    self.mouse_state.cancel_reenable();
}
```

commit.message:
[Pytorch] Add ignore_raw_node option to GraphPickler (#176939)

Summary:
The Graph Pickler raises "Unexpected raw Node during pickling" when it encounters a raw `torch.fx.Node` during serialization. This occurs in some backward graphs (e.g. IDXCVR model__27_backward_7) where metadata contains Node references that haven't been converted to `_NodePickleData`.

This diff adds an `ignore_raw_node` option to `GraphPickler.Options` that, when enabled, silently pickles unexpected raw Nodes as `None` instead of raising an `AssertionError`. This allows graph serialization to finish even when graphs contain these edge-case references.

Test Plan: buck test fbcode//mtia/accuracy/training/tests:test_graph_serialize; arc lint — no new issues

Reviewed By: aorenste

Differential Revision: D95510476

Pull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/176939
Approved by: https://github.com/aorenste

commit.message:
Distinct single/double quote annotations with nestings-based detection (#253)

Quote-context detection relied on checking the immediately preceding
token, which broke for multi-token quoted content. The single
`IsPartOfQuotedString` annotation made no distinction between `'...'`
and `"..."`, so both were colored identically.

## Changes

- **`TokenAnnotation`**: replaced `IsPartOfQuotedString` with
`IsPartOfSingleQuotedString` and `IsPartOfDoubleQuotedString`
- **`walk()` in `dparser.rs`**: quote context now determined by
`nestings.last()` rather than inspecting the previous token — more
robust and handles all tokens within a quoted region regardless of what
precedes them
- **`Palette`**: added `single_quoted_word()` → yellow,
`double_quoted_word()` → red
- **`token_to_style()`**: routes single-quoted tokens (including the `'`
delimiters) to yellow and double-quoted tokens to red

```rust
// Before: one annotation, both colored the same
IsPartOfQuotedString

// After: distinct per quote type, correctly colored
IsPartOfSingleQuotedString  // yellow
IsPartOfDoubleQuotedString  // red
```

commit.message:
Apply bash acceptance check when accepting fuzzy history entries (#249)

Pressing Enter on a fuzzy history entry blindly ran the command,
bypassing the `will_bash_accept_buffer` check used for normal Enter
presses. An incomplete history entry (e.g. `for i in 1 2 3; do echo $i`
without `done`) would be submitted immediately rather than dropping into
multi-line editing.

## Changes

- **Extract `try_submit_current_buffer`** — pulls the existing
Normal-mode submit logic (single-line check + `will_bash_accept_buffer`
gate) into a shared method
- **Fix fuzzy history Enter** — `FuzzyHistorySearch` Enter now calls
`accept_fuzzy_history_search()` then `try_submit_current_buffer()`
instead of unconditionally setting `ExitState::WithCommand`

```rust
// Before
ContentMode::FuzzyHistorySearch => {
    self.accept_fuzzy_history_search();
    self.mode = AppRunningState::Exiting(ExitState::WithCommand(
        self.buffer.buffer().to_string(),
    ));
}

// After
ContentMode::FuzzyHistorySearch => {
    self.accept_fuzzy_history_search();
    self.try_submit_current_buffer(); // same gate as normal Enter
}
```
